items.briefing:
A ransomware group called "The Gentlemen" has topped the rankings for the most active ransomware operations this quarter, according to a local security review — a reminder that ransomware remains a serious threat to Australian organisations including training providers. Separately, attackers are now hiding invisible text inside emails to trick AI-powered security filters into missing malicious content, with over one million such emails already detected. This technique could bypass your organisation's email security tools without you knowing.

With AI increasingly used in both attacks and defences, human oversight of automated security systems remains critical. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email asking you to click a link or open a file, report it to your IT team rather than acting on it — even if it passed your spam filter.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI security incidents shows urgent need for local defences.
